jokes.jokes: I am sometimes cold, sometimes warm, but never wet. I can fly without wings, flow without water, and cry without eyes. What am I?
What can you hold in your right hand, but never in your left hand?
What has words, but never speaks?
Why can you never trust an atom?
I'm always in motion, yet I never get tired. What am I?
As I walked along the path I saw something with four fingers and one thumb, but it was not flesh, fish, bone or fowl.
What comes once in a minute, twice in a moment, but never in a thousand years?
I’m tall when I’m young, and I’m short when I’m old. What am I?
The more you take out of me, the bigger I get. What am I?
Walk on the living, they don't even mumble. Walk on the dead, they mutter and grumble.
I come in a lot of different sizes. Sometimes, I drip a little. If you blow me, it feels really good. What am I?
You stick your poles inside me. You tie me down to get me up. I get wet before you do. What am I?
What four-letter word begins with “f” and ends with “k,” and if you can’t get it you can always just use your hands?
All day long it’s in and out. I discharge loads from my shaft. Both men and women go down on me. What am I?
I’m spread out before being eaten. Your tongue gets me off. Sometimes people lick my nuts. What am I?
First, think of the color of the clouds. Next, think of the color of snow. Now, think of the color of a bright, full moon. Now, answer quickly: What do cows drink?
What has a head, a tail, is brown and has no legs?
What breaks yet never falls, and what falls yet never breaks?
A man looks at a painting in a museum and says, “Brothers and sisters, I have none, but that man’s father is my father’s son.” Who is in the painting?
The person who makes it has no need for it; the person who buys it has no use for it. The person who uses it can neither see nor feel it. What is it?
What can you catch, but not throw?
What has a bottom at the top?
What asks no questions but must be answered?
What is red and smells like blue paint?
What gets smaller every time it takes a bath?
